text:
Once there was a curious squirrel named Oliver. Every day after breakfast, he'd race along the forest path, his tiny feet thunking against fallen leaves.

One crisp autumn morning, as he scampered after a chipmunk, he stumbled upon a patch of shining metal feathers. They glittered like stars. Oliver knew adventure! He picked them up, oh, so many.

Back home, he crafted a tiny hat. It was shiny too. Every time he looked at it, he'd imagine flying across the forest canopy.

His friendship with a wise old owl grew. "You're brightest feathered creature I know," the owl said. "But flying is hard..."

Yet each night Oliver flew dreams. Each swooped feelers grazed starlight.

One moonlit evening, Oliver soared far. The forest spun like a wheel. He reached a great oak. Its branches filled with birds singing. The birds sang a song of home.

Back down below, Oliver listened. Their songs were different
